case_name: Hollie Jackson, as Administrator of the Estate of Emma Hayes, and on Behalf of the Wrongful Death Beneficiaries of Emma Hayes v. Mayfield Ky Opco, LLC D/B/A Mayfield Health and Rehabilitation

summary.brief: Hollie Jackson, as Administrator of the Estate of Emma Hayes, and on Behalf of the Wrongful Death Beneficiaries of Emma Hayes v. Mayfield Ky Opco, LLC D/B/A Mayfield Health and Rehabilitation. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: On February 6, 2018, then-eighty-five-year-old Emma Hayes was admitted to Mayfield, a nursing home. Emma required twenty-four-hour monitoring and assistance with all aspects of daily life due to being wheelchair bound, her advanced age, and her numerous health conditions
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mayfield’s staff attempted to rouse Emma in order to administer her numerous medications but were unable to. Abandoning that effort, Mayfield’s staff took no action until nearly five hours later at 1:45 p.m. when they took her vitals.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Emma was accordingly transported by ambulance to Jackson Purchase Medical Center (JPMC), a local hospital. The ambulance’s patient care record stated that Emma’s level of distress was 'severe.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The emergency room records from JPMC indicate that Emma was in respiratory distress when she arrived at 2:38 p.m., that she was intubated at 2:58 p.m., and that she passed away at 3:40 p.m.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The circuit court erred by granting summary judgment. We accordingly reverse the Court of Appeals’ decision, vacate the circuit court’s summary judgment order, and remand for further proceedings consistent with this Opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Mayfield asserted, and the circuit court found in its order granting summary judgment, that Mayfield took Emma’s vitals at 9:51 a.m. on December 3 and found them to be normal. There is no evidence that this ever occurred based on any of Emma’s medical records that are now before this Court.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court dismissed all of Jackson’s claims with prejudice.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The circuit court erred in granting summary judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Jackson’s complaint asserted that Mayfield knew Emma was dependent upon it for twenty-four-hour care and that due to its ongoing and routine indifference for her basic care and needs she suffered respiratory failure, significant pain that went untreated, the accelerated deterioration of her health, unplanned weight loss and malnutrition, violations of her dignity, and death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Jackson filed the lawsuit at issue herein on November 9, 2021.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Difficulty breathing and altered mental status
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Emma Hayes, an 85-year-old female with multiple comorbidities, was found unresponsive at a nursing home. She had tested positive for COVID-19 a week prior and was transferred to a dedicated COVID unit.

focused_exam.general: Unresponsive with jerking/twitching of upper extremities and head

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Respiratory distress and altered mental status in a patient with multiple comorbidities

requestables.name: Chest X-ray
requestables.description: Chest X-ray to assess for acute cardiopulmonary disease

reveal.clinical_significance: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment
reveal.decision_impact: Could help identify causes of respiratory distress

timeline_phases.name: Nursing Home Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient found unresponsive at nursing home

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Department Workup
timeline_phases.description: Patient transported to hospital and assessed

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient found unresponsive with severe respiratory distress
decision_points.prompt: What immediate actions should be taken upon finding the patient unresponsive?

options.description: Immediately notify physician and transfer to hospital
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for significant change in condition
Inappropriate delay in care for critical condition

expected_action_court.description: Immediate notification and transfer to hospital
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony and standard emergency protocols

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in care led to worsening of condition
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate transfer to hospital for critical care
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient passed away shortly after hospital transfer

key_findings: Summary judgment was vacated and remanded for further proceedings
Material issues of fact regarding gross negligence

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate transfer could have altered outcome

factual_timeline.event: Emma Hayes admitted to Mayfield nursing home
Staff attempted to rouse Emma, found unresponsive
Vitals taken, patient in severe distress
Emma Hayes admitted to Mayfield Health and Rehabilitation
Emma Hayes found unresponsive and unable to take medication

allegations.allegation: Failure to timely transfer to hospital

court_findings.legal_significance: Immunity under COVID statute incorrectly applied

court_findings.finding: Material issues of fact regarding gross negligence
